mnemonics: My Very First App Loves Creating Activities (MVFA-LCA) - Represents the core components: My (Manifest), Very (View), First (FirstActivity), App (App), Loves (Layout), Creating (onCreate), Activities (Activities).
keyPoints: The first step is to set up the development environment with Android Studio and the SDK.
Every Android app must have a manifest file (AndroidManifest.xml) that declares its components and permissions.
The `onCreate()` method is the main entry point where you initialize your activity and set the content view.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to declare your Activity in the AndroidManifest.xml file, causing the app to crash on launch.
Trying to access a UI element (like a TextView or Button) with `findViewById()` before calling `setContentView()`, which results in a NullPointerException.
